elements.content: Evidence from 3 years of post-pandemic data

slides.title: Remote workers produce 13% more output per hour

elements.content: Stanford study (Bloom et al., 2023) tracked 16,000 workers over 9 months. Remote workers completed 13.5% more calls per shift, with fewer breaks and sick days.
Source: Bloom, N. et al. (2023). Does Working from Home Work? Quarterly Journal of Economics.

slides.title: The productivity gain compounds with reduced commute stress

elements.content: Average commute: 54 minutes/day (US Census Bureau, 2023). Remote workers reinvest 40% of saved commute time into work tasks. The remaining 60% goes to sleep and exercise, reducing burnout by 23%.
